name: Cefuroxime
executiveSummary: Second-generation cephalosporin. Treatment of upper and lower respiratory tract infections, otitis media, urinary tract, skin and soft tissues, bone and joint infections caused by Gram-positive bacteria and Haemophilus influenzae, Escherichia coli, and Klebsiella.
indications:
## Main indications

Second-generation cephalosporin. Treatment of upper and lower respiratory tract infections, otitis media, urinary tract, skin and soft tissues, bone and joint infections caused by Gram-positive bacteria and Haemophilus influenzae, Escherichia coli, and Klebsiella.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Vial 750 mg, 1,5 g IV; tablets 250–500 mg PO.
adulto.dose: IV: 750 mg–1,5 g every 8 h. PO: 250–500 mg every 12 h.

pediatrico.diluent: Sterile water for injection, 0.9% NaCl, 5% dextrose.
pediatrico.dose: 3 to 12 years: 75 -150 mg/kg/day every 8 h, maximum dose: 6 g/day; meningitis: 240 mg/kg/day , maximum dose: 9 g/day, >13 years and adults: 750-1500 mg/dose every 8 h, maximum dose: 6 g/day. Preoperative dose in adults: 1,5 g.
pediatrico.notes: Not recommended for meningitis due to Haemophilus influenzae. Adjust dose in renal insufficiency.

stability:
## General

- Diluted IV: 24 h refrigerated.

## Pediatric guide

- 24 h at room temperature and 48 h refrigerated between 4° C and 8° C.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Neutropenia, hemolytic anemia, headache, pseudomembranous colitis, phlebitis at injection site, rash, anal pruritus.

bibliography.citation: Hospital de Pediatría SAMIC Prof. Dr. Juan P. Garrahan. Institutional Pharmaceutical Formulary — cefUROxime (code 0041, ATC J01DC).
Institutional pediatric dilution and administration guide. June 2026.
